Harvard's women, from the Weld Boat House
The University of Pittsburgh Rowing Club (the Pitt Crew?)--more often than on the Ohio or Monongahela

The West Point Crew is at home there
Gonzaga's rowers, from the Charlotte Y. Martin Boathouse
Horsey-handling harness headgear
Suitable for consumption, or a THC-containing item made for consumption
It's an utter catastrophe! A situation of complete failure! It's this word from the French for "unbar"!
The works of Rabelais are described as this, a more elegant way to say "raunchy"

[IMAGE CLUE]
Seen here, it's a 15th century battle axe & pike mounted on a six-foot handle
Does the "ball" type of this phenomenon really exist, & if so, what exactly is it? Plasma, maybe?
Did a French plane make it across the Atlantic from Paris in 1927, 2 weeks before this American plane crossed in the other direction?
What happened to this man last seen in the parking lot of a Detroit area restaurant July 30, 1975?
Where is this captain's fabled treasure? Oak Island? Gardiner's Island? The Jersey Shore? Who knows?
What goes on inside this 2-word boundary of a black hole? (That's one we may never figure out)
This Rolling Stones song repeats, "Pleased to meet you, hope you guess my name" quite a few times
This Barry Manilow lass "kissed me & stopped me from shaking, & I need you today"
This Sheryl Crow song begins, "Life springs eternal on a gaudy neon street"
Moon Unit Zappa relates, "Like, my mother is, like, a total space cadet" in this 1980s song
Buddy Holly told us, "She's the one, I've been told, now, she's wearing a band of gold"
This alphanumeric IRS code section lets an employer make an account for a worker to deposit a tax-deferred part of their salary
It's a creditor's claim on a property; the involuntary type is put on a home if payment obligations have not been met
An account is "in" this 7-letter word if payments are overdue & default looms
Not as nice as an ATM, the AMT, or this, assures that an individual with a tidy income will pay at least something to Uncle Sam--right?
It's windy day fun, but it's also writing checks on insufficient funds & hoping late deposits will cover things before clearance

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Bobby Cannavale presents the clue.) In this movie, Al Pacino was Ricky Roma; in a 2012 revival of the play, I got to play the hotshot real estate salesman, Ricky, & Al was the desperate Shelley Levene

[IMAGE CLUE]
(James Corden presents the clue.) I guess it's got to be a revival when the play is well over 300 years old; memorable efforts in this royal Shakespeare role include Lee J. Cobb in 1968, Christopher Plummer in 2004 & Glenda Jackson in 2019

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Bobby Cannavale presents the clue.) It's not surprising that this work called "The Great American Tragedy" won the Tony for Best Play & also for 1984, 1999 & 2012 revivals. You know what's not getting revived? The title character

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Neil Patrick Harris presents the clue.) I made my Broadway debut in this David Auburn play about a mentally declining mathematician & his numbers-loving daughter; a 2026 revival has been announced & I wonder who will attempt my part of Hal

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Neil Patrick Harris presents the clue.) A play version of "Dracula" ran 261 performances, opening in 1927; fifty years later, after the movies made the count a household name, it ran almost a thousand starring this man in a role he was boooorn to play

final.clue: Phil Vischer, creator of this show, originally had a candy bar as the lead until his wife nudged him in a healthier direction
